rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/11/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: Original financial account statements (or original letters signed by an officer of the financial institution having the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance) must be filed to verify the ending balance of assets. Any difference between the statement(s) and the account must be reconciled. [LRSF 14.34 D. and Probate Code Sect. 2620(c)(5).] The number of shares and value of Metropolitan West Total shown to be on hand as of 6/18/22 appear to be incorrect, if the informational schedule showing the changes in the form or assets is accurate. This, in turn, carries over into the supplemental account, as reflected on the losses on sale schedule. No personal representative appears to have been appointed yet to whom the remaining estate would be transferred. (The order authorizing prior fees was dated 5/10/21; it was not filed until 5/11/21.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 1/3/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement (Added For Posting Of The Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 2] When a motion for determination of good faith settlement is uncontested, a "barebones" motion that sets forth the basis for the of good faith finding, accompanied by a declaration which sets forth a brief background of the case, is sufficient to support a good faith determination. The court need not engage in a weighing of the Tech-Bilt factors. (City of Grand Terrace v. Sup. Ct (1987) 192 Cal.App.3d 1251, 1261 ["[O]nly when the good faith nature of a settlement is disputed, it is incumbent upon the trial court to consider and weigh the Tech-Bilt factors. That is to say, when no one objects, the barebones motion which sets forth the ground of good faith, accompanied by a declaration which sets forth a brief background of the case is sufficient."]; see also Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Associates (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488, 499.) Here, Chicago Title Company has filed a motion setting forth the grounds of good faith of this settlement (MPA at 2:25-3:3). The Declaration of Tanner D. Brink, filed in support of the motion and signed under penalty of perjury, sets forth a brief background of the case and the settlement negotiations as they relate to Chicago Title Company. On the basis of the record before it, the court determines the settlement at issue in the motion was made in good faith. Chicago Title Company is ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with all substantive provisions of this tentative ruling, as published above, by the time set for this hearing. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the court day before the hearing. Remote appearance is available consistent with San Francisco Superior Court Local Rules, rule 14.1(D). To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. [Part 2 of 2]

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 2] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Joseph M. Quinn. Decedent Genette Trammell executed The Genette Trammell Living Trust UDT February 28, 2004 ("2004 Trust"). Just over a decade later, Genette executed a will ("2015 Will"), which revoked all prior wills and trusts. Genette died in late 2017. She owned real property known as 64 Potomac Street. Eventually, in PTR-18-302129, competing petitions for probate were filed. On March 19, 2021, and after a four-day trial, the court found the 2015 Will valid, and it ruled that the 2015 Will effectively revoked the earlier trust. The court appointed Floyd Trammell as executor. The 2015 Will left everything, including 64 Potomac Street, to Genette's disabled adult child, Donald Trammell. In the meantime, Lawrence Porchia, acting as trustee of the revoked 2004 Trust and in disregard of the 2015 Will, sold 64 Potomac Street to third parties in 2018. Pending before the court is a petition by executor Floyd Trammell to recover funds related to the sale; Chicago Title Company is referenced as the escrow agent on the sale of 64 Potomac Street in that petition. The executor and Chicago Title Company engaged in arms-length settlement negotiations and reached a settlement that is conditioned on a judicial good faith determination under Code of Civil Procedure section 877.6 et. seq. Now, Chicago Title Company moves for a good faith determination. The motion is unopposed. Chicago Title Company's motion for a good faith settlement determination is GRANTED. On its own motion, the court takes judicial notice of the following pleadings from this action: (1) Notice of Partial Conditional Settlement filed August 30, 2022 by Floyd Trammell, Executor for the Estate; (2) First Amended Petition for Return of Estate Property (Prob. Code section 850), filed January 31, 2022 by Floyd Trammell, Executor for the Estate; (3) Petition of Vanguard Properties, Inc. and Kilby Stenkamp for Equitable Indemnity, Contribution and Declaratory Relief, filed October 13, 2021 by Respondents Kilby Stenkamp and Vanguard Properties, Inc. (4) Petition for Return of Estate Property (Prob. Code section 850), filed September 21, 2021 by Floyd Trammell, Executor for the Estate; (5) Petition to Obtain Real and personal Property Under Probate Code Sections 850, 859, filed February 26, 2019 by Donald Trammell by and through his Guardian Ad Litem Clinton Killian. Notice is limited to the relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented and does not, for example, extend to the truth of assertions in pleadings or transcripts. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. UPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.) Under Code of Civil Procedure section 877.6, (a)(1) Any party to an action in which it is alleged that two or more parties are joint tortfeasors or co-obligors on a contract debt shall be entitled to a hearing on the issue of the good faith of a settlement entered into by the plaintiff or other claimant and one or more alleged tortfeasors or co-obligors, upon giving notice in the manner provided in subdivision (b) of Section 1005. [] (b) The issue of the good faith of a settlement may be determined by the court on the basis of affidavits served with the notice of hearing, and any counteraffidavits filed in response, or the court may, in its discretion, receive other evidence at the hearing. (c) A determination by the court that the settlement was made in good faith shall bar any other joint tortfeasor or co-obligor from any further claims against the settling tortfeasor or co-obligor for equitable comparative contribution, or partial or comparative indemnity, based on comparative negligence or comparative fault. (d) The party asserting the lack of good faith shall have the burden of proof on that issue. [] [Part 1 of 2]

rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Authorizing And Directing Payment Of Attorney'S Fees Incurred In Connection With Appointment Of Conservators, For Reimbursement From Conservatee'S Estate Of Attorney'S Fees Paid By Petitioner And For Reimburesent Of Conservatee'S Rent Advanced By Petitioner
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required by counsel to explain the court Prob. Code 2640.1 is applicable here. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JACK JAY CARLSON III
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, as notice was finally mailed directly to the decedent's mother. A declaration by an attorney purportedly representing the decedent's mother was itself insufficient to address the electronic service of notice to the mother without proof of written consent to electronic service signed by the mother, and the mother's inconsistent signatures on the three different waivers of bond were never addressed. Status of closing the estate is set for 12/11/2023. A petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration must be filed at least two weeks before 12/11/23. Appearance will be required on 12/11/2023 if no petition has been filed. No SASE was provided.

rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required for counsel to report the court if Petitioner(s) needs to be appointed as GAL for the beneficiary. If so, appears petition needs to be amended to mirror the petitioenrs' capacity to bring this petition. Also, Notice of Hearing [Judicial Council Form DE-120] to notify the agencies not used. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879.
